findings.says: Doc is structured as an LLM prompt brief ('You are a senior backend engineer...')
findings.actually: Implementation has shipped; docs/webhook-service-instructions.md is the actual current-state spec — two similarly named docs invite confusion
findings.fix: Rename to docs/webhook-service-prompt.md (or move to .planning/) and/or add a 'historical brief' banner pointing readers at webhook-service-instructions.md

findings.says: 113 markdown links flagged dead by find_dead_doc_links.py
findings.actually: All hits use GitHub-style #L<line> fragments (e.g. core/router.py#L99); the base files exist, only the local scanner cannot resolve line anchors. README, PRD, and docs/* are clean.
findings.fix: Either teach find_dead_doc_links.py to ignore #L\d+ fragments when base path resolves, or suppress the interview-prep/ directory

clean_areas: .env.example covers all 22 env vars read by core/config.py (prior HIGH fixed in PR #30 / commit 8ec48cb)
All 12 CLI subcommands dispatched in main.py are documented in README.md
PRD §13.3 Phase 3 row decomposed into shipped vs. todo sub-deliverables (prior HIGH fixed)
Python 3.9+ consistent across pyproject.toml, README.md, docs/setup-and-test.md
python-telegram-bot >=21.0, fastapi >=0.111.0, uvicorn >=0.30.0 in pyproject.toml match docs/webhook-service.md spec versions
GET /health and POST /telegram/webhook documented in README and docs/webhook-service-instructions.md match app.py routes
